title: Rupert Sheldrake Explains Extramission Theory!
summary: Rupert Sheldrake discusses extramission theory, explaining how visual perception may involve projecting images outward from the eye, and how this relates to mirror reflections and virtual images as described in physics textbooks.

key_claims.claim: When looking at a mirror, the viewer projects out images that go along with the light coming into the eye, but the virtual image projection goes straight through the mirror because it is virtual, not physical
The theory of sending images out from the eye, while considered heretical in mainstream science, is actually taught in physics textbooks to every school child through the concept of virtual images
